task_completion.description: Create a new Purchase Order record in iDempiere.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The iDempiere web interface is open to the 'Purchase Order' window with a new record being created.

task_completion.description: Set the vendor for the Purchase Order to 'Seed Farm Inc.'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Business Partner' or 'Vendor' field in the Purchase Order header is populated with 'Seed Farm Inc.'.

task_completion.description: Add the first order line for 50 units of Fertilizer #50 (or Compost).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Purchase Order Line tab shows a record with the product 'Fertilizer #50' (or 'Compost') and a quantity of 50.

task_completion.description: Add the second order line for 100 units of Mulch 10#.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Purchase Order Line tab shows a record with the product 'Mulch 10#' (or 'Mulch') and a quantity of 100.

task_completion.description: Save the Purchase Order and ensure it remains in Draft status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document status field (usually at the bottom or top of the header) explicitly shows 'Draft' or 'Drafted', and the 'Complete' or 'Process' button has not been executed.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual iDempiere web GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the iDempiere web application running in a browser, rather than terminal commands, API scripts, or database queries.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not mock the output in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are entered into genuine iDempiere form fields, not written out in a text editor, notepad, or terminal echo.

integrity.description: Verify the agent allowed default listed prices to populate.
integrity.visual_evidence: The price fields in the order lines contain populated numerical values (default prices) and were not manually bypassed, left blank, or spoofed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The document type is 'Purchase Order' and the required status is 'Draft'. The expected quantities are exactly 50 and 100. Note a known discrepancy in the test environment: the task asks for 'Fertilizer #50' but the database setup script uses 'Compost'. You must accept either 'Fertilizer #50' or 'Compost' for the first product. The second product is 'Mulch 10#' (accept 'Mulch' if the exact naming varies).
